creator: pav@FreeBSD.org
text:
State Changed
From-To: open->closed

This has been solved by teaching man to grok compressed includes, in all supported releases. 

http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=113096 

> This has been solved by teaching man to grok compressed includes, in all supported releases.

Yes and no... There still is an issue, related to catman:

With the MLINKS scheme that I proposed in this PR, we would receive
multiple hard-linked files that catman would correctly convert to
multiple catpages.

For instance:

# l /usr/local/man/man*1/xtrap*.1.gz
-r--r--r--  1 root  wheel  2577 Jul 31 20:46 /usr/local/man/man1/xtrap.1.gz
lrwxr-xr-x  1 root  wheel    10 Jul 31 20:46 /usr/local/man/man1/xtrapchar.1.gz@ -> xtrap.1.gz
lrwxr-xr-x  1 root  wheel    10 Jul 31 20:46 /usr/local/man/man1/xtrapin.1.gz@ -> xtrap.1.gz
lrwxr-xr-x  1 root  wheel    10 Jul 31 20:46 /usr/local/man/man1/xtrapinfo.1.gz@ -> xtrap.1.gz
lrwxr-xr-x  1 root  wheel    10 Jul 31 20:46 /usr/local/man/man1/xtrapout.1.gz@ -> xtrap.1.gz
lrwxr-xr-x  1 root  wheel    10 Jul 31 20:46 /usr/local/man/man1/xtrapproto.1.gz@ -> xtrap.1.gz
lrwxr-xr-x  1 root  wheel    10 Jul 31 20:46 /usr/local/man/man1/xtrapreset.1.gz@ -> xtrap.1.gz
lrwxr-xr-x  1 root  wheel    10 Jul 31 20:46 /usr/local/man/man1/xtrapstats.1.gz@ -> xtrap.1.gz
# 

Without MLINKS we get lots of compressed man pages that .so the "master"
manpage.

For instance:

# l /usr/local/man/man*1/xtrap*.1.gz
-r--r--r--  1 root  wheel  2577 Oct  2 17:24 /usr/local/man/man1/xtrap.1.gz
-r--r--r--  1 root  wheel    37 Oct  2 17:24 /usr/local/man/man1/xtrapchar.1.gz
-r--r--r--  1 root  wheel    37 Oct  2 17:24 /usr/local/man/man1/xtrapin.1.gz
-r--r--r--  1 root  wheel    37 Oct  2 17:24 /usr/local/man/man1/xtrapinfo.1.gz
-r--r--r--  1 root  wheel    37 Oct  2 17:24 /usr/local/man/man1/xtrapout.1.gz
-r--r--r--  1 root  wheel    37 Oct  2 17:24 /usr/local/man/man1/xtrapproto.1.gz
-r--r--r--  1 root  wheel    37 Oct  2 17:24 /usr/local/man/man1/xtrapreset.1.gz
-r--r--r--  1 root  wheel    37 Oct  2 17:24 /usr/local/man/man1/xtrapstats.1.gz
# zcat /usr/local/man/man1/xtrapchar.1.gz
.so man1/xtrap.1
# 

However, catman cannot process these correctly. Well, at least not on
7-STABLE, but I see no change in src/usr.bin/catman/catman.c towards
HEAD as well.

The verbose output from catman explains what's happening:

    format man1/xtrap.1.gz -> cat1/xtrap.1.gz
    format man1/xtrapchar.1.gz -> cat1/xtrapchar.1.gz
<standard input>:1: can't open `man1/xtrap.1': No such file or directory
    format man1/xtrapin.1.gz -> cat1/xtrapin.1.gz
<standard input>:1: can't open `man1/xtrap.1': No such file or directory
    format man1/xtrapinfo.1.gz -> cat1/xtrapinfo.1.gz
<standard input>:1: can't open `man1/xtrap.1': No such file or directory
    format man1/xtrapout.1.gz -> cat1/xtrapout.1.gz
<standard input>:1: can't open `man1/xtrap.1': No such file or directory
    format man1/xtrapproto.1.gz -> cat1/xtrapproto.1.gz
<standard input>:1: can't open `man1/xtrap.1': No such file or directory
    format man1/xtrapreset.1.gz -> cat1/xtrapreset.1.gz
<standard input>:1: can't open `man1/xtrap.1': No such file or directory
    format man1/xtrapstats.1.gz -> cat1/xtrapstats.1.gz
<standard input>:1: can't open `man1/xtrap.1': No such file or directory

Indeed the created catpages just contain whitespace. Which means that
It Does Not Work.

So either we go for MLINKS here (as the kernel sources do), or we teach
catman to correctly deal with .sourced compressed manpages.

My PR http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=96540 fixes this issue,
although it actually tackles a different problem. This PR is >3 years
old, but still open.

Kind regards,
Helge
